data.body:
PASS 1 review — [G1G2] Edit-set / scope accuracy — for the follow-up plan "Move .bridge_state/ + reconciler lock files off the shared tickets branch (conflict-free union)". (Recorded here alongside the other PASS-1 payloads on this epic.)

AFFIRMATIONS:
- All three files named in **Files** exist and are correct edit targets. `src/rebar/_commands/init.py` carries the committed `.gitignore`/`.gitattributes` scaffold (`_GITIGNORE` L37, `_GITATTRIBUTES` L49-57, `_commit_gitignore`/`_commit_gitattributes`/`_ensure_merge_ours_driver`). `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_advisory_lock.py` owns the lock/gate files (`_LOCK_FILE=".reconciler-pass-lock"` L61, `_GATE_FILE=".reconciler-phase-gate"` L62, written to the tickets branch via `_write_file_to_tickets_branch`). `src/rebar/_store/push.py` has the `.bridge_state` stash→merge→pop dance (L158-180) the plan proposes to simplify.
- High blast radius is acknowledged ("the reconciler currently coordinates cross-clone partly VIA these shared-branch files … needs its own design pass") — per G1G2, acknowledged blast radius is not a fail.

FINDINGS (un-enumerated consumers + one mis-attributed edit target):

- [ ] Correct the bindings-write attribution and add `reconcile.py` to **Files**: the plan says `_advisory_lock.py` "writes lock/gate + bindings to the shared branch", but `_advisory_lock.py` writes ONLY the `.reconciler-*` lock/gate files — `.bridge_state/bindings.json` (+ `bindings-retired.json`) is committed to the tickets branch by `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py::_commit_binding_store_snapshot` (`_rel_files` L237; commit block L1197-1230). That commit exists specifically so bindings survive `git merge origin/tickets` between passes (L1203-1213), so it is the durability mechanism the move must replace, not just an incidental writer.
  - criteria: G1G2 — location: plan **Files** / `_advisory_lock.py` line — evidence: `reconcile.py:237,1197-1213`; `_advisory_lock.py:61-62`

- [ ] Enumerate `src/rebar/_store/sync.py` as an edit/verify target: AC #2 ("union merge … conflict-free on all paths") is implemented in `sync.py::_do_reconverge`/`_union_merge` (`--allow-unrelated-histories`, L88-108), not in `push.py`. `sync.py` L19-20,90-91 explicitly depends on the `merge=ours` `.gitattributes` that AC #3 removes; its docstring/comments must change and it is where the conflict-free-union regression actually exercises.
  - criteria: G1G2 — location: plan **Files** — evidence: `sync.py:19-20,88-108`

- [ ] Enumerate the on-disk READERS of `.bridge_state/bindings.json` so a path change stays consistent: `src/rebar/_engine_support/resolver.py:50` and `binding_store.py:77,80,110` (the last bakes the path into a user-facing recovery hint `git show tickets:.tickets-tracker/.bridge_state/bindings.json`). The plan moves files off the committed branch but is silent on whether the on-disk worktree path changes; if it does, these readers break.
  - criteria: G1G2 — location: plan **Files** / **Scope** — evidence: `resolver.py:50`; `binding_store.py:77-80,110`

- [ ] Enumerate the external `git show tickets:.bridge_state/...` consumers that assume the file is committed: `scripts/retarget_jira_project.py` (L105-138) and `scripts/jira-pressure-test/e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:258` / `e2e_validation_probe.sh` restore bindings via `git show tickets:.bridge_state/bindings.json`. Gitignoring it off the branch makes those `git show` lookups fail — call out their fate.
  - criteria: G1G2 — location: plan **Scope / risk** — evidence: `retarget_jira_project.py:105-138`; `e2e_field_validation_probe.sh:258`

- [ ] Name the test artifacts the change must update so AC #3/#4 are reachable: `tests/interfaces/store/test_gitattributes_merge.py:50-51` asserts `.bridge_state/* merge=ours` and `.reconciler-* merge=ours` are committed — removing those lines (AC #3) breaks it directly. `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/state/test_binding_commit_survives_merge.py` (L127,174), `test_binding_retired_commit.py`, and `test_corrupt_state_guard.py` all assert bindings.json is committed-to/restorable-from the tickets branch; enumerate which are kept vs. redesigned.
  - criteria: G1G2 — location: plan **Acceptance Criteria** (#2/#3) — evidence: `test_gitattributes_merge.py:50-51`; `test_binding_commit_survives_merge.py:127,174`
